devdoc.details: Implements the dual-chain design from healthcare_ehr_scheme.md:   HISTORICAL CHAIN  — Immutable, stores verified EHR signature records.                        Every patient EHR upload that passes verification is logged.   EVIDENCE CHAIN    — Stores revocation evidence with Chameleon Hash.                        HA can update evidence content without changing the block hash. The actual ECC signature verification (σ_i · G == gpk_i + h₁·Ppub + h₂·KID_k + h₃·upk_i) is performed off-chain by the Python Flask consensus server (bcca/verify.py). Once verified, the server calls this contract to record the result on-chain. Role constants: 0 = PATIENT, 1 = DOCTOR, 2 = HOSPITAL_ADMIN

storeEHRRecord(string,string,string,stri.details: Called by the consensus server (Flask backend) ONLY after      bcca/verify.py confirms the signature σ_i is valid.

devdoc.title: BCCA_Healthcare

addEvidenceEntry(string,string,string,st.notice: Add a revocation evidence entry to the Evidence Chain.         CH_i value serves as the immutable block identifier.         Content can be updated by HA (Algorithm 10) via chameleon hash.

getEHRChainLength().notice: Returns list of EHR block hashes (to query the chain)

getPatientEHRs(string).notice: Get EHR records for a specific patient pseudonym

modifyEvidenceEntry(string,string,string.notice: Modify evidence data (Algorithm 10).         CH_i must remain unchanged — Python verifies chameleon hash property.         HA provides new credData that produces the same CH_i via trapdoor.

storeEHRRecord(string,string,string,stri.notice: Store a verified EHR signature record on the Historical Chain.

userdoc.notice: Blockchain-Based Certificateless Conditional Anonymous Authentication         for Healthcare EHR Sharing (Dual-Chain Architecture)
